message:
fix: suppress clippy lints for large error types, implicit hashers, and formatting

Address clippy warnings introduced by the new raptorq, cancel, and
web modules:

- cancel/symbol_cancel.rs: replace #[must_use] with
  #[allow(clippy::must_use_candidate)] on cancel() since the bool
  return is advisory not mandatory; remove stray blank line before
  from_bytes(); add #[allow(clippy::significant_drop_tightening)] on
  register_pending() where the lock guard is intentionally held
  across the entry-or-insert and push; fix hex literal grouping
  in test (0xfedcba9876543210 -> 0xfedc_ba98_7654_3210)

- raptorq/builder.rs: add #[allow(clippy::result_large_err)] on
  build() methods for both RaptorQSenderBuilder and
  RaptorQReceiverBuilder since the Error type is intentionally rich

- raptorq/pipeline.rs: add #[allow(clippy::result_large_err)] on
  send_object(), send_symbols(), receive_object(), poll_send_blocking,
  poll_flush_blocking, poll_next_blocking; add
  #[allow(clippy::cast_precision_loss)] and
  #[allow(clippy::cast_sign_loss)] on compute_repair_count(); merge
  duplicate Poll::Ready(Ok(())) and Poll::Pending arms in
  poll_flush_blocking

- web/extract.rs: add #[allow(clippy::implicit_hasher)] on four impl
  blocks that use HashMap<String, String> as a concrete type in trait
  implementations (Path, Query, Form, HeaderMap extractors)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
